title: Color Picker
shortDescription: Select a color and view its HEX, RGB and HSL values in one panel.
metaDescription: Select a color and see its HEX, RGB and HSL values in one panel for CSS, UI specs and brand work.
intro: Select a color or paste a HEX code to see its HEX, RGB and HSL values right away.
overview:
This tool is built for cases where one color needs to be checked in several formats without switching tools. It shows the selected color as HEX, RGB and HSL so you can copy the representation that fits your stylesheet, component spec or design note.

Use it when you are matching a brand color, preparing a UI handoff or verifying a theme token. The values update together, which makes it easier to compare shades and keep color references consistent across workstreams.
howItWorks: Choose a color with the picker or enter a HEX value.
The tool converts the color into matching RGB and HSL values.
Copy the format you need for CSS, documentation or component styling.

useCases.title: Theme token review
useCases.description: Check a token color in HEX, RGB and HSL before adding it to a design system or stylesheet.

useCases.title: Brand palette alignment
useCases.description: Verify that a brand color stays consistent when it needs to be shared across web, docs and design files.

useCases.title: Component state styling
useCases.description: Inspect a hover, active or focus color and capture the exact format used in the codebase.

examples.title: Primary button blue
examples.description: A vivid interface blue that needs exact values for button styles and token documentation.

examples.title: Neutral surface shade
examples.description: Useful for checking a dark neutral used in cards, overlays or text surfaces.

examples.title: Soft success green
examples.description: Handy when a design file stores the color in HSL but the stylesheet needs HEX or RGB.

faq.question: Can I enter a color in a format other than HEX?
faq.answer: Yes. If the tool accepts the format you paste, it will display the equivalent HEX, RGB and HSL values.

faq.question: Why do the values change when I move the picker slightly?
faq.answer: Each small movement selects a different shade, so the numeric values change immediately. That is normal and helps you capture the exact color point you need.

faq.question: What is the difference between RGB and HSL here?
faq.answer: RGB describes the red, green and blue channel mix. HSL shows the same color as hue, saturation and lightness, which can be easier when comparing tints and shades.

faq.question: Why do my copied values not look identical in another app?
faq.answer: Some apps round channels or interpret alpha and display settings differently. Always compare the actual numeric values, not the preview alone.
